DISCOVERY 610, NTC AND MTN TO COMPEL: INTERROGATORY RESPONSES; DOCUMENT PRODUCTION AND RESPONSES TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS CONTINUED FROM FEB-27-2009 TO TRIAL DEPT. MOTION AT MAR-04-2009, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 514. JUDGE: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED. PARTIES TO APPEAR AFTER THE MSJ HEARING.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MORGAN, LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/AND OR SUMMARY AJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED WHETHER PLAINTIFF PERFORMED THE CONTRACT AND THE REASONABLE VALUE OF PLAINTIFF'S SERVICES. THE COURT NOTES FOR THE RECORD AS FOLLOWS: REQUESTS TO JUDICIAL NOTICE GRANTED; ALL OBJECTIONS OVERRULED; THERE IS NO JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL. UNDERLYING FACTS IN DISPUTE; JUDGE WARD STRUCK THE FEE CLAIM BY OVER $7000,000; UNCLEAR WHETHER DEFENDANT'S INITIAL POSITION THAT FEES REASONABLE WAS THE RESULT OF IGNORANCE; WERE FEES STRUCK BECAUSE OF OVERBILLING?; COOPER DECLARATION SAYS FEES INFLATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759
ORDER ON PLAINTIFF MORGAN, LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANTS IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MORGAN, LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/AND OR SUMMARY AJUDICATION. Appearances: Brett Schuman, Esq. of Morgan, Lewis, (415) 442-1024, appearing for Plaintiff, and moving party; Jonathan Gregory, Esq. and Merri A. Baldwin, Esq. of Chapman, Popik & White LLP, (415) 352-3000, appearing for defendants, and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is Denied. A triable issue of fact is raised whether plaintiff performed the contract and the reasonable value of plaintiff's services. The Court notes for the record as follows: Requests to Judicial notice Granted; All objections overruled; There is no Judicial Estoppel. Underlying facts in dispute; Judge Ward struck the fee claim by over $7000,000; Unclear whether defendant's initial position that fees reasonable was the result of ignorance; Were fees struck because of overbilling?; Cooper Declaration says fees inflated. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
